note: Seed digest. Refreshed automatically each weekday morning by the scheduled task.

items.title: AI chip stocks rally: AMD +4%, Nvidia and Intel +2% on US–Iran peace deal
items.summary: Chip names jumped after a reported US–Iran agreement pushed oil lower and lifted equity futures. AMD is up ~130% YTD in 2026, far ahead of Nvidia's ~12%.

items.title: Micron leads chip group up 8% ahead of Q3 earnings on June 24
items.summary: Memory maker Micron rallies into its third-quarter print, with AI-memory demand in focus across the sector.

items.title: Nvidia posts record Q1 revenue of $81.6B, data-center up 92% YoY
items.summary: NVIDIA reported record revenue for the quarter ended April 26, 2026 — up 20% sequentially and 85% year over year — with data-center revenue of $75.2B.

items.title: Microsoft AI momentum builds across Azure and enterprise software
items.summary: Microsoft continues to capture AI-driven growth through Azure and enterprise products; analysts list it among June's top AI stocks alongside Nvidia, Broadcom, AMD and Alphabet.

items.title: White House executive order targets advanced AI innovation and security
items.summary: The June 2, 2026 order, 'Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security,' restates the administration's push for US AI leadership while addressing national-security risk.

items.title: Federal bill proposes 3-year preemption of state AI laws
items.summary: The 269-page 'Great American Artificial Intelligence Act' (introduced June 4) would preempt state AI rules including Colorado's, which takes effect June 30. The bill remains in committee.

items.title: Commerce eases chip-export review; 25% tariff on advanced AI chips in effect
items.summary: A January rule shifted H200/MI325X-equivalent licensing from 'presumption of denial' to case-by-case review, while a 25% import tariff on advanced AI chips reshapes supply-chain economics.

items.title: EU AI Act high-risk obligations begin applying August 2, 2026
items.summary: The bulk of the EU AI Act takes effect in August, adding transparency and evaluation duties for general-purpose models trained above 10^25 FLOPs — a compliance cost for large AI developers.

items.title: Markets jittery: Nasdaq's worst day of the year as Fed hike odds rise
items.summary: Earlier in June the Nasdaq fell 4.18% on rate-hike fears after strong jobs data. Consumer sentiment ticked up to 48.9 but 1-yr inflation expectations sit at 4.6%, keeping rate risk in focus.
